UserResumeProfile.title: UserResumeProfile
UserResumeProfile.description: Represents a user's overall profile within the CVForge application, linking to their created resumes. This entity holds metadata about the user's resume activity.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserResumeProfile entity.

currentResumeId.description: Reference to the currently active or last-edited Resume for this user. (Relationship: UserResumeProfile 1:N Resume)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Resume.title: Resume
Resume.description: Represents a single resume document created by a user, encapsulating all its content and chosen template.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Resume entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserResumeProfile who owns this resume. (Relationship: UserResumeProfile 1:N Resume)

title.description: A user-defined title for the resume (e.g., 'Software Engineer - Google').

templateId.description: Reference to the Template chosen for this resume's design. (Relationship: Template 1:N Resume)

objectiveSummary.description: A brief introductory statement or career objective for the resume.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the resume was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the resume was last updated.

ContactInformation.title: ContactInformation
ContactInformation.description: Stores the personal contact details to be displayed on a resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ContactInformation entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this contact information belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:1 ContactInformation)

firstName.description: The first name of the individual.

lastName.description: The last name of the individual.

phone.description: The primary phone number for contact.

stateProvince.description: The state or province of residence.

linkedinUrl.description: URL to the individual's LinkedIn profile.

portfolioUrl.description: URL to the individual's professional portfolio or personal website.

WorkExperience.title: WorkExperience
WorkExperience.description: Details about a single work experience entry on a resume, including job title, company, and responsibilities.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkExperience entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this work experience belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N WorkExperience)

jobTitle.description: The title of the position held.

companyName.description: The name of the company or organization.

location.description: The city and state/country where the job was located (e.g., 'San Francisco, CA').

startDate.description: The start date of the employment.

endDate.description: The end date of the employment. This field will be null if 'isCurrent' is true.

isCurrent.description: Indicates if this is the user's current or most recent job.

bulletPoints.description: An array of bullet points describing responsibilities and achievements in this role. This is where AI-generated content can be stored.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this work experience entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this work experience entry was last updated.

Education.title: Education
Education.description: Details about a single educational background entry on a resume, including institution, degree, and dates.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Education entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this education entry belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Education)

institutionName.description: The name of the educational institution.

degree.description: The degree or qualification obtained (e.g., 'Bachelor of Science', 'Master of Arts').

major.description: The field of study or major.

location.description: The city and state/country where the institution is located.

startDate.description: The start date of the education period.

graduationDate.description: The date of graduation or expected graduation.

gpa.description: The Grade Point Average (GPA), if applicable.

honorsAwards.description: An array of honors or awards received during this educational period.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this education entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this education entry was last updated.

Skill.title: Skill
Skill.description: Represents a specific skill or competency of the user to be listed on a resume.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Skill entity.

resumeId.description: Reference to the Resume this skill belongs to. (Relationship: Resume 1:N Skill)

name.description: The name of the skill (e.g., 'Python', 'Project Management', 'UI/UX Design').

category.description: An optional category for the skill (e.g., 'Programming Languages', 'Soft Skills', 'Tools & Technologies').

level.description: An optional indicator of proficiency level (e.g., 'Beginner', 'Intermediate', 'Advanced', 'Expert').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this skill entry was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this skill entry was last updated.

Template.title: Template
Template.description: Defines a resume template, including its visual style and metadata, available for users to select.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Template entity.

name.description: The display name of the template (e.g., 'Modern Blue', 'Classic Professional').

description.description: A brief description of the template's style and features.

thumbnailUrl.description: URL to a thumbnail image preview of the template.

styleConfiguration.description: A string containing the style or layout configuration data for rendering this template (e.g., JSON blob, CSS rules, or internal identifier for a rendering engine).

isActive.description: Indicates if the template is currently available for users to select.

definition.description: Stores the single resume profile for a specific user. The 'id' field within the document should match the 'userId' from the path, ensuring direct ownership. This path provides authorization independence as the `userId` wildcard directly links to `request.auth.uid`.

Represents a single resume owned by a user. The 'userProfileId' field within the document must match the 'userId' from the path for robust authorization. This path embeds the owner's ID for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the resume document.

definition.description: Stores contact details for a specific resume. The 'resumeId' field within the document must match the 'resumeId' from the path. Authorization is inherited from the parent resume path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent resume.

params.name: contactInformationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the contact information document.

definition.description: Stores individual work experience entries for a specific resume. The 'resumeId' field within the document must match the 'resumeId' from the path. Authorization is inherited from the parent resume path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent resume.

params.name: workExperienceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the work experience document.

definition.description: Stores individual education entries for a specific resume. The 'resumeId' field within the document must match the 'resumeId' from the path. Authorization is inherited from the parent resume path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent resume.

params.name: educationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the education document.

definition.description: Stores individual skill entries for a specific resume. The 'resumeId' field within the document must match the 'resumeId' from the path. Authorization is inherited from the parent resume path.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent resume.

params.name: resumeId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent resume.

params.name: skillId
params.description: The unique identifier for the skill document.

definition.description: Stores predefined, publicly accessible resume templates. These documents are read-only for all users.

params.name: templateId
params.description: The unique identifier for the template document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed following a strict hierarchical model, primarily based on user ownership. Each authenticated user will have a dedicated subtree under `/users/{userId}/` for all their personal resume data. This approach maximizes security, simplifies rule writing, and inherently supports Query Atomicity Guarantees (QAPs).

**Authorization Independence:** This design achieves authorization independence by embedding the owner's `userId` directly into the path for all user-specific data. For instance, a user's resume profile is located at `/users/{userId}/userProfile`, and their individual resumes at `/users/{userId}/resumes/{resumeId}`. All sub-entities of a resume (contact information, work experience, education, skills) are stored as subcollections under their respective resume, like `/users/{userId}/resumes/{resumeId}/workExperiences/{workExperienceId}`. This means that any security rule for reading or writing a document can solely rely on comparing `request.auth.uid` with the `{userId}` wildcard in the path, without needing to perform `get()` operations on parent documents to determine ownership or permissions. For enhanced data integrity and self-contained documents, the `userProfileId` field in `Resume` documents will explicitly store the `userId` from the path, and `resumeId` fields in sub-collection documents will store the `resumeId` from their respective paths, acting as robust invariants.

**Query Atomicity Guarantees (QAPs):** The structural segregation strategy directly supports QAPs. All user-specific data is contained within the `/users/{userId}/` path, ensuring that a `list` operation on a collection like `/users/{userId}/resumes` will inherently only return resumes owned by the `request.auth.uid`. Public, static data, such as `Template` entities, are stored in a top-level collection (`/templates`). These are globally readable, allowing for simple `allow read: true` rules. There is no mixing of public and private data in the same collection, eliminating the need for complex, inefficient, or insecure rules that attempt to filter results post-query.
